1â†’# HW06 - Extended Visitor Ballworld + List Visitors
     2â†’
     3â†’## Design Pattern
     4â†’
     5â†’This assignment introduces two major concepts:
     6â†’
     7â†’1. **Extended Visitor Pattern**: Ball objects become typed "hosts" (DefaultBall,
     8â†’   FollowerBall, LeaderBall, GangsterBall) that accept "visitor" algorithms
     9â†’   (`IBallAlgo`). Each visitor can define different behavior for different ball
    10â†’   types, enabling type-dependent processing without `instanceof` checks.
    11â†’
    12â†’2. **List Visitor Framework**: Functional-style list processing using the Visitor
    13â†’   pattern. Lists are either empty (`MTList`) or non-empty (`NEList`), and
    14â†’   algorithms (`IListAlgo`) provide `emptyCase` and `nonEmptyCase` methods.
    15â†’
    16â†’## Architecture Overview
    17â†’
    18â†’### Ballworld with Extended Visitors
    19â†’
    20â†’```
    21â†’Controller
    22â†’â”œâ”€â”€ BallModel (model)
    23â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ IDispatcher<IBallCmd>
    24â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Typed Ball Hosts (IBall extends IBallHost<IBall>)
    25â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ DefaultBall - standard ball
    26â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ DynamicBall - changeable type (new, to implement)
    27â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ FollowerBall - follows leaders
    28â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ LeaderBall - attracts followers
    29â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ GangsterBall - chaotic behavior
    30â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ Ball Visitors (IBallAlgo<R, P>)
    31â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ AConfigBallAlgo - abstract config algo with model adapter
    32â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ OneTypeBallAlgo - same behavior for all types (to implement)
    33â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ SameTypeBallAlgo - type-specific config (to implement)
    34â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ LeadConfigAlgo - leader-follower dynamics
    35â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ FishWorldConfigAlgo, SolarSystemConfigAlgo, etc.
    36â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ Each defines per-type cases for Default, Follower, Leader, Gangster
    37â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ Update/Paint/Interact strategies (from HW05)
    38â†’â””â”€â”€ BallView (view)
    39â†’```
    40â†’
    41â†’### List Visitor Framework
    42â†’
    43â†’```
    44â†’IList (abstract list)
    45â†’â”œâ”€â”€ MTList (empty list, singleton)
    46â†’â””â”€â”€ NEList (non-empty list, has first + rest)
    47â†’
    48â†’IListAlgo (visitor interface)
    49â†’â”œâ”€â”€ emptyCase(MTList host, Object... params)
    50â†’â””â”€â”€ nonEmptyCase(NEList host, Object... params)
    51â†’```
    52â†’
    53â†’## What Is Provided
    54â†’
    55â†’### Ballworld
    56â†’
    57â†’- **`IBall.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallHosts/IBall.java`): Extended ball
    58â†’  interface. Extends both `IObserver<IBallCmd>` and `IBallHost<IBall>`. Includes
    59â†’  `getCount()`, `setCount()`, `getRandGen()`, `interactWith()`, and all
    60â†’  getter/setter methods from HW05.
    61â†’- **`ABall.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallHosts/ABall.java`): Abstract ball
    62â†’  extending `ABallHost<IBall>`. Provides `move()`, `bounce()`, `paint()`,
    63â†’  `update()`, `updateState()`, and `interactWith()` implementations. Has an
    64â†’  internal `count` clock and `IRandomizer`.
    65â†’- **`DefaultBall.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallHosts/DefaultBall.java`): Provided.
    66â†’  Standard ball type with `IBallHostID ID`.
    67â†’- **`FollowerBall.java`**, **`LeaderBall.java`**, **`GangsterBall.java`**:
    68â†’  Provided. Each has a unique `IBallHostID ID` for visitor dispatch.
    69â†’- **`IBallAlgo.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/IBallAlgo.java`): Visitor
    70â†’  interface extending `IBallHostAlgo<R, P, IBall>`.
    71â†’- **`BallAlgo.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/BallAlgo.java`): Concrete
    72â†’  visitor with host-ID-keyed case dispatch.
    73â†’- **`AConfigBallAlgo.java`** (`src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/AConfigBallAlgo.java`):
    74â†’  Abstract config algo with convenience methods `installUpdateStrategy()`,
    75â†’  `installPaintStrategy()`, `installInteractStrategy()`.
    76â†’- **`LeadConfigAlgo.java`**: Reference implementation showing how to define
    77â†’  per-type behavior in a visitor.
    78â†’- **All update/paint/interact strategies** from HW05 (ported to HW06 packages).
    79â†’
    80â†’### List Framework
    81â†’
    82â†’- **`IList`**, **`MTList`**, **`NEList`**: Provided by `provided.listFW.*`.
    83â†’  `IList.execute(IListAlgo algo, Object... params)` dispatches to the visitor.
    84â†’- **`IListAlgo`**: Visitor interface with `emptyCase` and `nonEmptyCase`.
    85â†’- **`ListDemoApp.java`** (`src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/controller/ListDemoApp.java`):
    86â†’  Demo controller that tests list algorithms.
    87â†’- **Helper visitors** `LargestAcc`, `LastAcc`, `ContainsAcc` in the visitors package.
    88â†’
    89â†’## What You Implement
    90â†’
    91â†’### Part A: Ballworld Extended Visitors
    92â†’
    93â†’#### 1. `DynamicBall` -- `src/hw06/model/BallHosts/DynamicBall.java` (new file)
    94â†’
    95â†’Create a new ball host type that can dynamically change its type ID at runtime.
    96â†’It should extend `ABall` and have:
    97â†’- Its own `IBallHostID ID`
    98â†’- A constructor matching the pattern of `DefaultBall`, `FollowerBall`, etc.
    99â†’- Optionally a method to change its host ID dynamically
   100â†’
   101â†’#### 2. `OneTypeBallAlgo` -- `src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/OneTypeBallAlgo.java` (new file)
   102â†’
   103â†’A configuration algorithm where all ball types (Default, Follower, Leader,
   104â†’Gangster) receive the **same** configuration. The default case installs the
   105â†’same update strategy, paint strategy, and interact strategy regardless of ball
   106â†’type. Extend `AConfigBallAlgo`.
   107â†’
   108â†’#### 3. `SameTypeBallAlgo` -- `src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/SameTypeBallAlgo.java` (new file)
   109â†’
   110â†’A configuration algorithm where ball behavior depends on the ball type. Define
   111â†’specific cases for each ball host ID:
   112â†’- **DefaultBall**: Basic configuration (e.g., straight movement, ball paint)
   113â†’- **FollowerBall**: Follows leaders (e.g., attracted to LeaderBalls)
   114â†’- **LeaderBall**: Leads followers (e.g., distinct paint, movement pattern)
   115â†’- **GangsterBall**: Aggressive behavior (e.g., different interaction)
   116â†’
   117â†’Use `addCmd()` on the `BallAlgo` to register cases for each `IBallHostID`.
   118â†’
   119â†’### Part B: List Visitor Exercises
   120â†’
   121â†’All list algorithms are in `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/`
   122â†’and implement `IListAlgo`.
   123â†’
   124â†’#### 4. `LargestFwdAlgo` -- Forward Accumulation: Find Largest
   125â†’
   126â†’Find the largest integer in a list using forward accumulation.
   127â†’- `emptyCase`: Return `Integer.MIN_VALUE`
   128â†’- `nonEmptyCase`: Pass `host.getFirst()` as the initial accumulator to a helper
   129â†’  visitor. The helper compares each element to the accumulator and keeps the max.
   130â†’
   131â†’#### 5. `LastFwdAlgo` -- Forward Accumulation: Find Last Element
   132â†’
   133â†’Find the last element using forward accumulation.
   134â†’- `emptyCase`: Return `null`
   135â†’- `nonEmptyCase`: Pass `host.getFirst()` to a helper that replaces the
   136â†’  accumulator with each new element. At the empty list base case, return the
   137â†’  accumulator (which is the last element seen).
   138â†’
   139â†’#### 6. `ContainFwdAlgo` -- Forward Accumulation: Check Membership
   140â†’
   141â†’Check if a given value exists in the list.
   142â†’- `emptyCase`: Return `false`
   143â†’- `nonEmptyCase`: The search value comes via `params[0]` (as a String to parse).
   144â†’  Use a boolean accumulator that becomes `true` once the element is found.
   145â†’  OR the current result with each comparison.
   146â†’
   147â†’#### 7. `FromNthAlgo` -- Sum from Nth Position
   148â†’
   149â†’Sum all elements starting from the Nth position (0-indexed or 1-indexed based
   150â†’on implementation).
   151â†’- `emptyCase`: Return `0`
   152â†’- `nonEmptyCase`: Decrement a counter (passed as `params[0]`). Only add
   153â†’  `host.getFirst()` to the sum once the counter drops below 0 (past the Nth
   154â†’  position). Recurse on `host.getRest()`.
   155â†’
   156â†’#### 8. `RemoveSmallestRevAlgo` -- Reverse Accumulation: Remove Smallest
   157â†’
   158â†’Return a new list with the smallest element removed (single pass).
   159â†’- `emptyCase`: Return `MTList.Singleton`
   160â†’- `nonEmptyCase`: Compare `host.getFirst()` against the minimum of the rest.
   161â†’  If current element is the minimum, skip it; otherwise, prepend it and recurse
   162â†’  on the rest.
   163â†’
   164â†’## File Paths (Editable)
   165â†’
   166â†’| File | Package | What to implement |
   167â†’|------|---------|-------------------|
   168â†’| `src/hw06/model/BallHosts/DynamicBall.java` | `hw06.model.BallHosts` | New ball host type |
   169â†’| `src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/OneTypeBallAlgo.java` | `hw06.model.BallVisitors` | Uniform config algo |
   170â†’| `src/hw06/model/BallVisitors/SameTypeBallAlgo.java` | `hw06.model.BallVisitors` | Type-specific config algo |
   171â†’| `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/LargestFwdAlgo.java` | `...visitors` | Find largest (forward) |
   172â†’| `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/LastFwdAlgo.java` | `...visitors` | Find last (forward) |
   173â†’| `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/ContainFwdAlgo.java` | `...visitors` | Check membership (forward) |
   174â†’| `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/FromNthAlgo.java` | `...visitors` | Sum from Nth (mixed) |
   175â†’| `src/visitorDemoExercises/listFWVisitorExercises/model/visitors/RemoveSmallestRevAlgo.java` | `...visitors` | Remove smallest (reverse) |
   176â†’
   177â†’## Expected Behavior
   178â†’
   179â†’### Ballworld
   180â†’
   181â†’- **DynamicBall**: A ball that can change its host type dynamically, allowing
   182â†’  visitors to process it differently over time.
   183â†’- **OneTypeBallAlgo**: Creates balls where all types look and behave identically,
   184â†’  demonstrating that the visitor can apply uniform configuration.
   185â†’- **SameTypeBallAlgo**: Creates balls where Followers, Leaders, and Gangsters
   186â†’  each have distinct appearances and behaviors, demonstrating type-dependent
   187â†’  visitor dispatch.
   188â†’

Now I understand the full picture. Let me implement all the required files.

**Summary of what needs to be implemented:**

1. **DynamicBall.java** - `setHostID()` and `execute()` methods
   - `setHostID(hostID)` - sets the local hostID field
   - `execute()` - uses `hostID` (the local one) to call `algo.caseAt(hostID, this, params)`

2. **OneTypeBallAlgo.java** - `reset()`, `isDefaultRunFunc()`, and `setType()` methods
   - `reset(isDefaultRunFunc)` - if isDefaultRunFunc, set default cmd to runFuncCmd; otherwise use defaultCmd. Also set currentID to defaultID.
   - `isDefaultRunFunc()` - returns true if defaultCmd == runFuncCmd (effectively checking if the default case is set to run func)
   - `setType(ballTypeID)` - removes the old type cmd (if not default ID), sets new type to runFuncCmd; if old type is defaultID, reset default to defaultCmd; if new type is defaultID, set default to runFuncCmd

3. **SameTypeBallAlgo.java** - The default case needs to get the current host's ID and compare it against the "other" ball's ID to determine the matching logic.

The approach is to create an inner algo keyed with the host's ID that returns true, then dispatch on the other ball to check if its type matches.

Now I'm implementing the list algorithms, starting with `LargestFwdAlgo` which uses a helper that accumulates values through forward traversal, returning the largest value found.
